channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I got tired of wiring up the same boilerplate every project — so I built this
item.description: fullstack-launchpad A production-ready full-stack starter kit — React + Node.js + PostgreSQL + Docker + CI/CD. Clone it, configure it, ship it. The problem Every time you start a new project, you...

item.title: Is a Data Science Bootcamp Worth It in 2026?
item.description: If you’re asking data science bootcamp worth it, you’re probably trying to buy speed: a faster path to job-ready skills than a drawn-out degree or random tutorials. The uncomfortable truth is that...

item.title: Stop trying to replace code reviewers. Brief them.
item.description: Stop trying to replace code reviewers. Brief them. In the last 18 months, AI changed how code gets written. It also broke how it gets reviewed — and most of the industry is fixing the wrong thing....

item.title: The best thing about Next 2026? The people.
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge The opinions in this article are all mine. Any resemblance to persons living or dead is purely coincidental. I usually write about...

item.title: Give Your AI Agent Real-Time Product Data with BuyWhere
item.description: AI agents are getting better at reasoning, but they still fail on a basic commerce task: answering product questions with current prices and availability. Ask an agent, "What is the cheapest...

item.title: Reddit Subreddit Tracker 深度测评：批量追踪 Reddit 热门内容
item.description: Reddit Subreddit Tracker 深度测评：批量追踪 Reddit 热门内容，一键提取 engagement 信号 做出海产品、搞科研分析、盯市场舆情——你可能都需要从 Reddit 提取某个 Subreddit 的热门讨论。 Reddit 本身的搜索和排序功能用起来不够顺手，多个 Subreddit 同时监控更是麻烦。今天推荐一个可以批量追踪 Subreddit...

item.title: Privacy-first mind mapping app. Part 4: Zero Knowledge and Private Thought
item.description: This chapter explains why zero knowledge is not marketing decoration for MindMapVault. It is a product boundary. It also explains the limits that come with that choice: no admin rescue, no silent...

item.title: [SC] Usar un ejecutor de actor personalizado
item.description: Preguntas ¿En qué situaciones el executor por defecto de Swift resulta insuficiente y cuándo debería considerarse un ejecutor personalizado? No se quiere usar el pool de hilos global cooperativo....

item.title: Building Recipe-Finder.org: A Full-Stack Journey with Vue, Express, MongoDB, and Vuetify 🍳
item.description: Hello, DEV community! 👋 Today, I want to share a project I recently launched: Recipe-Finder.org. Like many developers, I often find myself staring into the fridge wondering what to make with the...

item.title: The Future of Autonomous Innovation: Inside the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform|Google Cloud Next '26
item.description: The landscape of artificial intelligence is shifting from static models to dynamic, autonomous entities. At Google Cloud Next '26, the unveiling of the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform marked a...

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign...

item.title: Researchers say we’re talking less than ever
item.description: Researchers at the University of Missouri-Kansas City and the University of Arizona say that between 2005 and 2019, the number of words we speak out loud to another human being fell by nearly 28...

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding...

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: Sharge’s fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
item.description: I’ve been testing compact, magnetic Qi2 power banks that can snap onto your phone for an upcoming buying guide. They make recharging much easier than bringing along a huge battery that weighs down...

item.title: The Govee smart lamp brightened up my room, and then my life
item.description: I knew things were not quite right when I had to throw a towel over a broken Ikea lamp to block out its light. How did I get here? I cover fancy and capable tech for a living, and yet, it took me...

item.title: Xbox’s weirdest studio is on a roll
item.description: For a while there, it seemed like Double Fine might be struggling under the Microsoft corporate umbrella. The game studio led by Tim Schafer is beloved for offbeat titles like Brütal Legend and...

item.title: Sho Miyake answers life’s greatest questions
item.description: Acclaimed Japanese director Sho Miyake has arrived in the States. He's brought with him two feature films: Small, Slow But Steady and Two Seasons, Two Strangers, a pair of naturalistic portraits...

item.title: The US gets the worst phones
item.description: Apple and Samsung dominate the US phone market, and they've done so for years. Together with Google, they've shaped our sense of what a smartphone is and what it can do, pushing the boundaries of...

item.title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

item.title: Ace the Ping-Pong Robot Can Whup Your Ass
item.description: Ace can read the trajectory of a ball, adjust the racket angle, and respond with strokes that keep the exchange alive with real players.

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment...

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its...

item.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
item.description: On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: How Squid Survived Extinction Events
item.description: Science news: Scientists have finally cracked a long-standing mystery about squid and cuttlefish evolution by analyzing newly sequenced genomes alongside global datasets. The research reveals that...

item.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
item.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

item.title: Microsoft will let you pause Windows Updates indefinitely, 35 days at a time
item.description: Windows users will no longer be forced to run automatic updates in the middle of a game or a busy day. Microsoft is rolling out some long-awaited changes to Windows Update to users on its Dev and...

item.title: Alex Jones has uncovered another massive conspiracy
item.description: Alex Jones may soon lose Infowars, the digital perch that he's used for decades to traffic in conspiracy theories, to The Onion, the satirical newspaper. But not before he's uncovered something...

item.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
item.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.
